settings.title: A Simpler Way to Stop Skipping Leg Day
settings.excerpt: If your lower body always loses to fatigue, the fix is usually about order, not motivation.
settings.body:
Leg day gets skipped more than any other session, and it is rarely a discipline problem. Usually it is sitting at the end of a tiring week when you have nothing left.

The simplest fix is to move it. Put your leg session at the start of your training week, when you are fresh and the gym is quieter.

Order beats willpower

Most people try to fix a skipped session by promising to want it more. That rarely works for long. Changing when the session sits in your week is a structural fix, and structural fixes hold up better than motivation on a Friday evening.

Start with one big lift you can measure, like a squat or a leg press, and add a small amount over time. Finish with a couple of easier movements rather than a marathon of machines.

A session you can actually finish

Keep the plan short enough that finishing it feels easy. A workable leg day might be:

  • One main lift, taken to a few solid working sets.
  • One supporting movement for the back of the legs.
  • One easy finisher, then home.

Done first and kept short, leg day stops being the session you dread. Ask any of the floor team to help you build the order that suits you.
